Communications Services Down on Deal Skepticism - Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies fell amid skepticism about deal activity. Shares of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros.

Discovery fell following reports that Paramount Chief Executive David Ellison and his father, Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, are lining up private-equity backers for a bid that could run as high as $60 billion.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1740ET

data.title: Tech Down as AI Frenzy Cools - Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies slipped as the artificial-intelligence frenzy cooled off.

The Roundhill Magnificent Seven exchange-traded fund, which tracks many of the big players in AI, fell, but remain roughly 37% higher over the last 12 months.

Investors are concerned that capital expenditure on AI infrastructure has become a reckless arms race, with apparently "circular" cross-investments between chip makers, data-center builders and Big Tech software makers muddying the demand picture.

Alphabet's Google will pump about $5.82 billion into Brussels, the capital of capital into Belgium over the next two years, the latest large-scale investment from a U.S. tech giant bolstering its data-center infrastructure in Europe.

Microsoft recently unveiled similar multibillion-dollar AI investment plans in the United Kingdom.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1740ET

data.title: AWS Sponsor Spotlight: AI in Entertainment
data.description:
Samira Bakhtiar, General Manager of Media & Entertainment, Games & Sports Business, AWS, speaks with Bloomberg moderator about cloud innovation at Bloomberg Screentime in Los Angeles.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Financials Down as First Brands' Worries Linger - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions declined amid fears that a messy auto-parts bankruptcy could point to broader issue in credit markets.

BlackRock reportedly requested cash withdrawal from a Jefferies Financial fund exposed to the First Brands' bankruptcy. Shares of Jefferies had plunged Wednesday, and compounded those losses with another slip Thursday.

Raistone, a trade-finance firm also caught up in the bankruptcy, requested an outside examination of its collapse and how $2.3 billion in assets "simply vanished."

HSBC Holdings made a $13.6 billion offer to buy out other shareholders in Hang Seng Bank, a smaller Hong Kong lender that it has long controlled, as the global lender turns its focus to its domestic market.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1738ET

data.title: Consumer Cos Down Despite Pepsi, Delta Strength - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies slipped as a vacuum of economic data offset generally strong earnings.

Delta Air Lines rallied after the Atlanta carrier said a recent resurgence in demand for tickets is set to continue, and highlighted the success of a strategy to sell more premium seating.

PepsiCo tapped a Walmart executive, Steve Schmitt, to fill its top finance role as the packaged-food company aims to turn around its slowing soda and snack sales following modest third-quarter revenue growth.

Japanese convenience-store chain 7-Eleven's same-store retail fuel volume declined by 4.8% year to year in the fiscal second quarter.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1738ET

data.title: Health Care Down as Deal Activity Offset by Regulatory Fears - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked down as deal activity was offset by regulatory fears.

Danish obesity-drug maker Novo Nordisk agreed to acquire Akero Therapeutics for up to $5.2 billion in cash.

Shares of Akero rallied, while Novo Nordisk retreated.

Pfizer shares continued a recent retreat during which the Big Pharma company has given back a large chunk of the gains associated with its compromise on tariffs.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1738ET

data.title: Industrials Down on Shutdown Fears - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies fell sharply amid fears that a government shutdown would weigh on U.S. economic activity.

A pair of Wall Street firms estimate that initial jobless claims rose slightly to 224,000 in the week ended Sept. 27 from 218,000 in the prior week, as the labor market appeared to hold up ahead of the recently commenced government shutdown.

GM is bringing back its Chevrolet Bolt with a new model that it expects to account for much of its electric-vehicle volume next year.

Italian sports-car maker Ferrari's shares slumped after new long-term profitability targets missed expectations.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1738ET

data.title: Materials Down as Dollar Gains - Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell as the U.S. dollar gained against rivals.

Silver futures tested the $50-per-ounce level for the first time since the Hunt brothers cornered the market on the commodity in the 1980s, amid growing investor demand for safe havens and persistent supply shortfalls.

Gold futures fell 2.4% from record highs after a peace deal in Gaza quelled some geopolitical fears.

China tightened its control over foreign markets for its rare-earth minerals, crucial to making high-tech and defense equipment, in a move that could reignite trade tensions with the U.S. ahead of an expected meeting between President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

Shares of American rare-earth producers USA Rare Earth and MP Materials rose on the prospect of more backing from the U.S. Lithium producers also rose amid reports China would tighten requirements for companies to export certain lithium batteries.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1737ET

data.description:
Grupo México, S.A.B. de C.V. (BMV:GMEXICO B) made a binding offer to acquire Grupo Financiero Citibanamex, S. A. de C. V. from Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) for $9.3 billion on October 6, 2025. A cash consideration of $9.3 billion will be paid by Grupo México, S.A.B. de C.V. As part of consideration, $9.3 billion is paid towards common equity of Grupo Financiero Citibanamex, S. A. de C. V.
Grupo México, S.A.B. de C.V. (BMV:GMEXICO B) cancelled the acquisition of Grupo Financiero Citibanamex, S. A. de C. V. from Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) on October 9, 2025

data.title: Novabay Pharmaceuticals says David Lazar resigns as CEO
Amcor plc Appoints Stephen R. Scherger as Executive Vice President, Effective November 10, 2025
data.description:
Amcor plc announced the appointment of Stephen R. Scherger as Executive Vice President, effective November 10, 2025. Mr. Scherger succeeds Michael Casamento, who after ten years as Amcor's Chief Financial Officer has decided to return home to Australia to be closer to his family on a full-time basis and will leave the Company as a result. Mr. Casamento will remain as an advisor to the Company until June 30, 2026, to help support a smooth transition.

Mr. Scherger brings more than 30 years of finance, operations and strategy experience in the packaging industry. Most recently, he served as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer of Graphic Packaging since 2015, where he played a leading role in transforming Graphic Packaging into the world's largest producer of fiber-based packaging, including supporting the integration and synergy realization for a number of large-scale acquisitions. During his tenure, Graphic Packaging's net sales more than doubled to nearly $9 billion and net income nearly tripled.

From 2015 to 2025, Mr. Scherger served as Chief Financial Officer of Graphic Packaging (NYSE: GPK), a publicly traded provider of fiber-based packaging solutions to food, beverage, foodservice, and other consumer products companies. From 2014 to 2015 he served as Senior Vice President, Finance of Graphic Packaging and from 2012 to 2014, he served as Senior Vice President, Consumer Packaging of Graphic Packaging, where he had direct responsibility for a $2.1 billion business comprising approximately 6,000 employees and 28 manufacturing facilities. He spent the first 25 years of his career at MeadWestvaco, where he served in finance, operations and strategy roles of increasing responsibility, ultimately assuming the role of President of the company's $1.3 billion Beverage and Consumer Electronics business.

Mr. Scherger is a member of the Board of Directors of Middleby Corporation, serving as a member of the Audit Committee and Chair of the Compensation Committee.

data.description:
Hollywood is typically focused on the people in front of the camera - and, increasingly, that means streamers like Imane Anys, aka "Pokimane," one of the most popular streamers on Twitch with nearly 10 million dedicated followers. She joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."

data.title: Salesforce to Acquire Software Firm Apromore
data.description:
By Dean Seal


Salesforce said it has agreed to acquire Apromore, a provider of process intelligence software, in a deal with undisclosed financial terms.

The customer-relationship management platform said Thursday that adding Apromore's suite of process intelligence capabilities will accelerate its vision for agentic process automation, or the use of AI agents to complete high-level business tasks.

"Apromore gives customers the end-to-end visibility they need to understand how their business truly operates," Salesforce Chief Product Officer Steve Fisher said.

The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026.


Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

Seeing Early Signs of Silver Outperforming Gold: Citi's Layton
data.description:
Spot silver prices jumped to the highest level in decades as surging demand for safe-haven assets ran into supply constraints in the London bullion market. Silver has been boosted this year by the so-called "debasement trade," with investors flocking to the perceived safety of Bitcoin, gold and silver. Gold also hit a record this week as it passed $4,000 at one point. On "Bloomberg Markets," Scarlet Fu talks with Max Layton, global head of commodities research at Citi (C), about the surge in prices and his price targets.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires
